derived_from.hypothesis: A direct user command to change the weekly plan should trigger a strategy.md update_context call instead of a follow-up confirmation question.

intent: When the user explicitly changes weekly training volume in chat, the assistant should propose a strategy.md Weekly Plan edit and then reply briefly.

context.me:
# Profile

- Adam, 42M, 81 kg.
- Wife + 3-year-old; live in Crosshaven, Ireland.
context.strategy:
# Strategy

## Goals — Current focus

- Hold 3 runs + 2 lifts/week at 15+ km volume.
- Build tempo capacity: extend tempo segments toward 3 km @ sub-5:10/km.

## Weekly Plan

**Week runs Monday → Sunday.** Target: 3 runs + 2 strength sessions per week (~15 km+ total running).

- Run days: ~5 km each
  - 1 tempo session/week when HRV is above personal baseline (~50 ms): tempo segments of 1.5–2.5 km @ 5:05–5:15/km pace
  - Remaining runs at easy pace (5:30–6:00/km)
- Strength A: Push + core
- Strength B: Pull + core
- Prefer not to stack run + strength on the same day
context.log:
# Log

- 2026-04-09 [strength b done] — slept better, felt strong

turns.content: How is my trend for running in last 2 months?
Your running over the last 2 months is trending clearly faster.
Change my plan to 4 runs per week

assertions.name: does_not_ask_to_lock_it_in
patterns: Want me to lock this in

notes: Real regression derived from thumbs-down feedback #12 / llm_call #304. The failed reply proposed a new 4-run structure but did not call update_context, instead asking whether to lock it in. This case pins the missing strategy update behavior.
